data.title: GREEN: Implement generate-skill-eval.sh
data.description:
## Description

Implement plugins/dso/scripts/generate-skill-eval.sh — a script that reads a skill SKILL.md and produces a starter evals/promptfooconfig.yaml with TODO-marked llm-rubric assertions.

## Implementation Steps

1. Parse skill name from $1 argument; show usage on --help and exit 0
2. Resolve DSO_SKILLS_ROOT (default: $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills)
3. Validate skill directory exists — exit 1 with "ERROR: Skill directory not found: <path>" if missing
4. Check for existing evals/promptfooconfig.yaml — exit 1 with "ERROR: evals/promptfooconfig.yaml already exists for skill '<name>'" if present
5. Parse SKILL.md: try frontmatter description: field first (between --- delimiters), then fall back to first H2 section body. H2 extraction must be fence-aware: track ``` markers and skip H2 lines inside code blocks. Exit 1 with "ERROR: No parseable description found in SKILL.md for skill '<name>'" if neither found.
6. Escape YAML special characters (colons, quotes, newlines, pipes, ampersands) in extracted description
7. Generate YAML from this template:

```yaml
description: "<skill-name> skill evals"

providers:
  - id: "anthropic:messages:claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"

defaultTest:
  options:
    provider: "anthropic:messages:claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"

prompts:
  - |
    You are the <skill-name> skill.
    <ESCAPED_DESCRIPTION>

    The user says:
    {{prompt}}

tests:
  - description: "TODO: Verify <skill-name> handles basic input correctly"
    vars:
      prompt: |
        TODO: Replace with a representative input for the <skill-name> skill
    assert:
      - type: llm-rubric
        value: >
          TODO: Replace with evaluation criteria derived from the skill
          description: <ESCAPED_DESCRIPTION_SUMMARY>

  - description: "TODO: Verify <skill-name> follows behavioral constraints"
    vars:
      prompt: |
        TODO: Replace with an input that tests a behavioral constraint
    assert:
      - type: llm-rubric
        value: >
          TODO: Replace with behavioral constraint verification criteria
          derived from: <ESCAPED_DESCRIPTION_SUMMARY>
```

8. Write to temp file (mktemp), then mkdir -p evals/ and mv temp → evals/promptfooconfig.yaml (atomic write)
9. Print success message to stdout: "Generated evals/promptfooconfig.yaml for skill '<name>'"

## All parsing logic lives in this single script (no separate library extraction needed — single consumer).

## File Impact
- CREATE: plugins/dso/scripts/generate-skill-eval.sh

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `bash tests/run-all.sh` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] `ruff check` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py
- [ ] Script exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/generate-skill-eval.sh
- [ ] All RED tests from Task 0b5a-86cc now pass (GREEN)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-generate-skill-eval.sh
- [ ] --help flag shows usage and exits 0
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/generate-skill-eval.sh --help
- [ ] Error messages include skill name/path and are actionable
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/generate-skill-eval.sh nonexistent-skill 2>&1 | grep -q "nonexistent-skill"
